_comment: Single source of truth for the published TASE.2 point model and the HMI. Each station is one PLC/feed; each point is one published ICCP indication point. 'type' real -> RealQ float point (WRITEF), state -> StateQ integer point (WRITEI). 'name' is the ICCP point name the server publishes and the ingest tag database writes to (the join key between this file and ingest/tags*.json). A point may add 'control' to make it commandable from the HMI: control.kind 'discrete' for a state point (operator picks a state) or 'setpoint' for a real point (operator sends a value). The server then also creates a TASE.2 Block 5 device control object named <point>_ctl; the HMI operates it, and the ingest reads it and writes the command down to the PLC. The server builds its model from these names+types, the bridge subscribes and groups the HMI by station, and the HMI renders one card per station.
